source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The transformer shown in the figure has an input voltage of 220 V and can output voltages of 12 V, 18 V, and 30 V. The primary coil with \( n_1 \) turns has a voltage varying with time as \( \mu = U_{\mathrm{m}} \cos (100 \pi t) \). A single-turn coil wound around the iron core is connected to an AC voltmeter, which shows a reading of 0.1 V. When a resistor \( R \) with a resistance of 12 \(\Omega\) is connected across points \( B \) and \( C \), the power is 12 W. Which of the following statements is correct?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: If \( R \) is connected across \( A \) and \( C \), the current through \( R \) is 2.5 A, with a period of 0.02 s

choice_map.A: \( n_1 \) is 1100 turns, and \( U_{\mathrm{m}} \) is 220 V
choice_map.B: The number of turns in the coil between \( B \) and \( C \) is 120, and the current through \( R \) is 1.4 A
choice_map.C: If \( R \) is connected across \( A \) and \( B \), the voltage across \( R \) is 18 V, with a frequency of 100 Hz
choice_map.D: If \( R \) is connected across \( A \) and \( C \), the current through \( R \) is 2.5 A, with a period of 0.02 s

multi_step: Solving requires combining several relations: transformer voltage-turn ratio, the single-turn voltmeter reading of 0.1 V, and the resistor power relation P=V^2/R or P=I^2R
The time-varying primary voltage mu = Um cos(100πt) adds a frequency/period inference step that must be checked against the choices
This is a 4-choice correctness evaluation problem where the solver must derive multiple intermediate quantities before selecting the final statement
verifiability: The answer space is constrained by 4 explicit options, and answer_text matches one full option statement directly
The claims to verify are numeric/discrete quantities such as turns, voltage, current, frequency, and period, which are amenable to rule-based checking

open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: The transformer shown in the figure has an input voltage of 220 V and can output voltages of 12 V, 18 V, and 30 V. The primary coil with \(n_1\) turns has a voltage varying with time as \(\mu = U_{\mathrm{m}} \cos(100\pi t)\). A single-turn coil wound around the iron core is connected to an AC voltmeter, which shows a reading of 0.1 V. When a resistor \(R\) with a resistance of 12 \(\Omega\) is connected across points \(B\) and \(C\), the power is 12 W. If \(R\) is connected across points \(A\) and \(C\), what is the period of the current in seconds?
